column2: A system that allows for the sharing of identity information across multiple independent security domains or organizations.
The entity in a federation that authenticates the user and issues identity assertions/tokens.
The entity in a federation that provides the service or resource, relying on the IdP's authentication.
An XML-based framework for exchanging authentication and authorization data between an IdP and SP.
The XML document sent from an IdP to an SP containing statements about a user's identity and permissions.
An industry-standard protocol for delegated authorization (uses Access Tokens).
An authentication layer built on top of OAuth 2.0 (uses ID Tokens).
A mechanism allowing a user to authenticate once and gain access to multiple systems without re-authenticating.
The automatic creation of a user account in a service provider the first time a user logs in via federation.
A compact, URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties; commonly used in OIDC.
